title: A prompt-cache experiment finds 30-second keepalives waste money
friendlyTitle: AI agents may be paying too much to keep prompts cached
summary: A measured comparison across Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini and DeepSeek found that the common 30-second cache keepalive was eight times more frequent than necessary. In the tested ten-minute pauses, a roughly four-minute interval saved money for Anthropic, improved cache reliability without dollar savings for DeepSeek, and brought little benefit for OpenAI or Gemini.
friendlySummary: An experiment found that sending a cache message every 30 seconds is usually wasteful and that each AI provider behaves differently.
whyItMatters: Long-running agents repeatedly resend large conversation prefixes, so a small timer choice can become a material API bill. Builders should measure each provider’s cache behaviour and stop keepalives when their cumulative read cost exceeds simply rebuilding the cache.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Teams can lower agent costs by measuring the real cache lifetime and using a slower timer only where it saves money.
